title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 Artificial Intelligence Act
authority: European Parliament and Council of the European Union
source: Official Journal of the European Union
summary: Comprehensive risk-based EU law governing AI systems and general-purpose AI models, with obligations phased in after entry into force.
auditUse: Use for regulatory scoping, high-risk classification, technical documentation, risk management, human oversight, post-market monitoring, and GPAI compliance.

title: General-Purpose AI Code of Practice
summary: Voluntary code intended to help providers comply with the AI Act's general-purpose AI obligations.
auditUse: Use to inspect GPAI provider evidence around transparency documentation, copyright policy, systemic-risk evaluation, incident reporting, and security controls.

title: Guidelines for Providers of General-Purpose AI Models
summary: Commission guidance explaining how providers should understand and meet AI Act obligations for general-purpose AI models.
auditUse: Use when testing provider/deployer role classification, downstream information duties, and evidence expectations for GPAI model governance.

title: Guidelines on Classification of High-Risk AI Systems
summary: Draft Commission guidance clarifying when AI systems should be classified as high-risk under the AI Act.
auditUse: Use as a scoping aid when determining whether Annex III or product-safety-linked AI systems trigger high-risk obligations.

title: AI Pact
summary: Voluntary initiative for organizations preparing for AI Act implementation before mandatory obligations apply.
auditUse: Use to compare a firm's pre-compliance commitments against actual implementation evidence.

title: Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law
summary: International treaty framework focused on AI impacts on human rights, democracy, and the rule of law.
auditUse: Use for public-sector, human-rights, and democratic accountability audits where national implementation may reference the Convention.

title: Executive Order 14179: Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence
summary: Current U.S. executive policy directing agencies to support American AI leadership and revise federal AI memoranda.
auditUse: Use as context for federal AI governance posture, especially when auditing U.S. government AI adoption or procurement.

title: Winning the AI Race: America's AI Action Plan
summary: Federal AI policy roadmap organized around innovation, infrastructure, and international leadership.
auditUse: Use to understand U.S. federal priorities that may shape agency AI adoption, procurement, and AI infrastructure reviews.

title: OMB M-25-21: Accelerating Federal Use of AI through Innovation, Governance, and Public Trust
summary: OMB policy for federal agency use of AI, replacing earlier federal AI governance guidance with revised expectations.
auditUse: Use to test agency AI inventories, governance roles, high-impact AI reviews, risk practices, and public transparency artifacts.

title: OMB M-25-22: Driving Efficient Acquisition of Artificial Intelligence in Government
summary: OMB policy for federal acquisition of AI, focused on procurement practices and adoption of AI technologies.
auditUse: Use for AI vendor due diligence, acquisition documentation, contract-risk review, and procurement control testing.

title: Colorado Artificial Intelligence Act: SB24-205 Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence
summary: Colorado law governing developers and deployers of high-risk AI systems with duties around algorithmic discrimination and consumer protections.
auditUse: Use to scope high-risk AI systems, risk-management programs, impact assessments, notices, and documentation for Colorado-facing deployments.

title: NYC Local Law 144: Automated Employment Decision Tools
authority: New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection
summary: NYC law requiring bias audits and public notices for certain automated employment decision tools used in hiring or promotion.
auditUse: Use as a concrete audit model for AEDT bias-audit scope, independence, public posting, notice, and selection-rate evidence.

title: Utah Artificial Intelligence Policy Act: SB 149
summary: Utah law addressing generative AI disclosures in regulated occupations and consumer interactions, with a state AI policy office and learning laboratory.
auditUse: Use to check disclosure controls, consumer-facing GenAI workflows, and sandbox/exception documentation for Utah-facing services.

title: California AB 2013: Generative AI Training Data Transparency
summary: California law requiring covered generative AI developers to publish training-data transparency documentation.
auditUse: Use to test whether model cards, dataset summaries, copyright/data-source disclosures, and publication timing meet transparency expectations.

title: California SB 942: California AI Transparency Act
summary: California law requiring covered generative AI providers to include provenance disclosures for certain AI-generated content.
auditUse: Use when auditing synthetic-media provenance, content labeling, metadata preservation, and user-facing disclosure controls.

title: Illinois Artificial Intelligence Video Interview Act
summary: Illinois law governing employer use of AI analysis in video interviews, including notice, consent, and limits on sharing.
auditUse: Use for employment-AI audits involving applicant consent, deletion workflows, data sharing, and transparency notices.

title: Directive on Automated Decision-Making
summary: Mandatory Canadian federal policy for automated decision systems used in administrative decisions.
auditUse: Use to evaluate algorithmic impact levels, peer review, notice, explanation, testing, monitoring, and release documentation in Canadian federal systems.

title: Algorithmic Impact Assessment
summary: Mandatory risk assessment tool supporting Canada's Directive on Automated Decision-Making.
auditUse: Use as a practical questionnaire and evidence scaffold for public-sector AI impact assessment.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Data Act Companion Document
authority: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
summary: Government companion document explaining the proposed Artificial Intelligence and Data Act.
auditUse: Use for tracking Canadian private-sector AI regulatory proposals and likely evidence expectations for high-impact systems.

title: A Pro-Innovation Approach to AI Regulation: Government Response
authority: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
summary: UK government response setting out a non-statutory, regulator-led approach based on cross-sector AI principles.
auditUse: Use to understand UK expectations around safety, transparency, fairness, accountability, contestability, and sector-specific regulator plans.

title: Interim Measures for the Administration of Generative AI Services
authority: Cyberspace Administration of China and other authorities
summary: Chinese regulation for public-facing generative AI services, including content, data, transparency, and oversight expectations.
auditUse: Use for auditing public-facing GenAI services in China for provider duties, data provenance, labeling, complaint channels, and safety controls.

title: Provisions on the Administration of Algorithmic Recommendations in Internet Information Services
authority: Cyberspace Administration of China and other authorities
summary: Chinese provisions regulating algorithmic recommendation services, including provider governance and user controls.
auditUse: Use when auditing recommender systems for filing, transparency, harmful-content mitigation, user profiling controls, and opt-out mechanisms.

title: Provisions on the Administration of Deep Synthesis of Internet-based Information Services
authority: Cyberspace Administration of China, MIIT, and Ministry of Public Security
summary: Chinese rules for deep synthesis services, including labeling, technical management, and misuse controls.
auditUse: Use for synthetic-media audits involving voice, image, video, virtual-scene, and deepfake generation services.

title: Measures for Labeling AI-Generated Synthetic Content
authority: Cyberspace Administration of China and other authorities
summary: Chinese measures requiring identification and labeling of AI-generated or synthetic content.
auditUse: Use when auditing explicit labels, implicit machine-readable labels, provenance metadata, and downstream content-handling controls.

title: Basic Act on the Development of Artificial Intelligence and Creation of a Foundation for Trust
summary: South Korea's comprehensive AI law establishing national AI policy, trust foundations, and requirements for certain AI systems.
auditUse: Use for Korea-facing AI governance scoping, high-impact AI duties, transparency obligations, and trust-management controls.

title: Introducing Mandatory Guardrails for AI in High-Risk Settings
authority: Department of Industry, Science and Resources
summary: Australian proposal for mandatory guardrails for AI in high-risk settings.
auditUse: Use to monitor likely Australian compliance expectations around high-risk AI, accountability, transparency, testing, and human oversight.

title: PL 2338/2023: Brazilian Artificial Intelligence Legal Framework
summary: Brazilian bill proposing a risk-based legal framework for AI governance, rights, and accountability.
auditUse: Use to track Brazilian AI regulatory direction and anticipated risk-classification and rights-impact evidence.

title: India AI Governance Guidelines
authority: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
summary: Indian government guidelines for safe and trusted AI innovation and governance.
auditUse: Use for India-facing governance reviews where sectoral oversight, trusted AI principles, and institutional coordination are relevant.

title: The Bletchley Declaration
authority: Countries attending the AI Safety Summit 2023
summary: Non-binding international declaration recognizing frontier AI risks and the need for international cooperation.
auditUse: Use as context for frontier AI safety governance, model evaluation commitments, and international risk-management expectations.

title: Frontier AI Safety Commitments
summary: Voluntary frontier AI commitments by major AI companies announced around the AI Seoul Summit.
auditUse: Use to evaluate frontier model safety frameworks, risk thresholds, external testing, and deployment-stop commitments.
